_meta.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
_meta.purpose: Machine-readable classification of every scientific claim in the Higgins Unity Framework, assigned to the HIVIP promotion ladder. Each claim carries its current level, the evidence that supports it, and the specific conditions required for promotion or demotion. This is the honesty layer for the entire programme.
_meta.methodology: HIVIP — Higgins Iterative Validation and Integrity Protocol
_meta.anti_corruption_rule: No concept climbs the ladder by improving one domain at the cost of contaminating earlier DUTs or the core signal.
_meta.integrity_rule: Prefer a weaker but cleaner claim over a stronger contaminated one.
_meta.update_protocol: When a claim is promoted or demoted, update this file with the date, evidence, and reason. History is preserved in the changelog array. This file is part of the refresh chain.

three_tier_system.tier_1_genuinely_strong: Mathematically sound and empirically credible. Ready for external review.
three_tier_system.tier_2_promising_not_proved: Interesting, plausible, worth pursuing. Not yet at evidential level for strong claims.
three_tier_system.tier_3_exploratory_atlas: Generates hypotheses. Should not be presented as proof of universality.

chatgpt_review_integration.mapping_to_hivip: Tier 1 maps roughly to L4-L5. Tier 2 maps to L1-L3. Tier 3 maps to L0-L1. The mapping is approximate — HIVIP and ChatGPT's tiers measure different things (HIVIP = promotion readiness, ChatGPT = external credibility).

discipline_framing.definition: The study of how ordered compositions behave under lawful aggregation, coarse-graining, and contamination on the simplex.
discipline_framing.key_sentence: EITT is not the theory itself. It is the first instrument in a broader discipline of compositional constraint diagnostics.
discipline_framing.pll_position: PLL/Rayleigh language is currently the leading engineering interpretation, not yet its final proof-theoretic foundation.

L0.name: exploratory_pattern
L0.definition: Interesting visual, analogy, or domain-specific signal. May inspire work, but does not yet influence core claims.

L1.name: candidate_overlay
L1.definition: Testable add-on readout that may rank or explain cases, but does not alter the science signal.

L2.name: process_control_candidate
L2.definition: Methodological addition that can improve integrity, detection, or transparency, but must survive HIVP regression.

L3.name: validated_companion
L3.definition: A robust secondary instrument that adds information while remaining separate from the core observable.

L4.name: canonical_method_component
L4.definition: A promoted method element that has survived cross-domain validation and regression through prior DUTs.

L5.name: core_science
L5.definition: The non-negotiable primary claim of the programme.

EITT_entropy_invariance.claim: Shannon entropy of compositional time series is near-invariant under geometric-mean block decimation across temporal resolutions.
EITT_entropy_invariance.chatgpt_note: Shannon entropy variation under geometric decimation: 0.18% at 341:1 compression. Empirical measurement.
EITT_entropy_invariance.justification: This is the primary scientific claim of the entire programme. Validated across 8+ domains (European prices 0.18% at 341:1, EMBER 6 countries mean 1.02%, NGFS 35 scenarios geometric 1.8%, geochemistry 37/39 TAS types, Gold/Silver 0.60%, US Energy 5/5 interior PASS, QGP freeze-out 0.27%, neutron star correctly FAILS). Adversarial testing: 10/17 pass, 7 fail as expected (synthetic/shuffled). Two sealed experiments confirm.

sealed_experiments: EXP-02 (US Energy): 5/5 interior PASS, 5/5 boundary FAIL as designed
evidence.adversarial: 10/17 pass, 7 fail (correct rejections of synthetic/shuffled data)

open_points: General mathematical theorem still incomplete — empirical invariance established, formal proof partial (Hessian bound covers second order)
Exact tightness of higher-order terms in high-D near-boundary cases
promotion_history: Cross-domain validation through HIVP regression chain
Sealed in EXP-01 and EXP-02 at FIXED POINT v3.2
demotion_conditions: Discovery of a legitimate compositional time series with strong temporal autocorrelation where EITT fails by >5%
Proof that the Hessian bound is not tight and the true bound permits >5% deviation in typical conditions
EITT_entropy_invariance.contamination_risk: LOW — the observable is defined cleanly, uses standard CoDa operations, and the operating envelope is documented

geometric_mean_superiority.claim: Geometric mean is the correct averaging operation on the simplex; arithmetic mean destroys compositional structure. EITT provides empirical proof.
geometric_mean_superiority.chatgpt_note: Under ILR transform, geometric means become arithmetic means. This makes geometric block decimation the correct operator for averaging compositions in Aitchison geometry. The 40-year gap filled.
geometric_mean_superiority.justification: This is implicit in Aitchison geometry (1982) and is the primary gift to the CoDa community. EITT provides the FIRST measurable consequence: geometric 5yr mean 1.8% vs arithmetic 5yr mean 14.2% (NGFS data). The CoDa community has argued this theoretically for 40 years; EITT provides empirical ammunition.

evidence.ngfs_comparison: Geometric 5yr: 1.8% mean, 4.7% max. Arithmetic 5yr: 14.2% mean, 28.6% max. Factor of 8× worse.
evidence.sealed_experiments: EXP-01 and EXP-02 both use geometric mean decimation exclusively for EITT verification
evidence.theoretical_basis: Aitchison (1982/1986), Egozcue & Pawlowsky-Glahn — geometric mean IS the Fréchet mean in Aitchison geometry

open_points: The theoretical argument is established; EITT adds the measurable consequence. No open points on the claim itself.
promotion_history: Inherited from CoDa literature as theoretical property
EITT provided first measurable empirical proof April 2026
demotion_conditions: Discovery that arithmetic mean preserves entropy equally well in some legitimate compositional domain — would undermine the uniqueness claim
geometric_mean_superiority.contamination_risk: NONE — this claim rests on 40 years of established CoDa theory plus new empirical evidence

simplex_closure_and_coda_geometry.claim: Compositional data lives on a simplex. The Aitchison geometry (CLR, ILR, ALR transforms, perturbation, powering) is the correct mathematical framework for analysis.
simplex_closure_and_coda_geometry.chatgpt_note: The mathematical substrate is real and well-established (Aitchison 1986, Pawlowsky-Glahn 2015). Standard mathematics.
simplex_closure_and_coda_geometry.justification: Inherited directly from Aitchison (1982/1986) and the established CoDa literature. Not a HUF claim — HUF adopts it. Every HUF operation is built on this foundation.

evidence.literature: Aitchison (1982, 1986), Egozcue et al. (2003), Pawlowsky-Glahn & Egozcue (2001), Tolosana-Delgado, van den Boogaart
evidence.huf_validation: Every sealed experiment verifies closure to machine precision and CLR zero-sum

promotion_history: Established science since 1982. Adopted by HUF, not claimed by HUF.
demotion_conditions: Not applicable — this is established mathematics, not a HUF-specific claim

operating_envelope.claim: EITT is reliable when the composition has sufficient diversity (Shannon entropy H > 1.0) and low zero rates (< 15%). When >35% of observations are zero, multiplicative replacement introduces artifacts that EITT correctly detects.
operating_envelope.chatgpt_tier: Tier 1 — genuinely strong (blind test: 17/20, 90% sensitivity, 80% specificity)
operating_envelope.chatgpt_note: Commendably explicit about the three misses. Two have plausible internal fixes. Right scientific posture.
operating_envelope.justification: Established by EXP-02 with deliberate design: 4 interior states PASS (H > 1.0, zero rate < 6%), 4 boundary states FAIL (H < 0.7, zero rate > 37%), 2 bridge states at the crossover. The 1% threshold separates cleanly. F17 tuner provides early warning.

evidence.exp02_sealed: 5 PASS / 5 FAIL exactly as designed. 1% threshold is a real boundary.
evidence.boundary_states: Rhode Island (8.8%), West Virginia (5.3%), Wyoming (2.8%), Delaware (6.4%) — all fail by 3-9× threshold
evidence.interior_states: California (0.83%), Minnesota (0.49%), Texas (0.21%), Wisconsin (0.13%) — all pass with margin
evidence.bridge_case: Pennsylvania: geometric FAILS at 1.09%, arithmetic PASSES at 0.87% — the exact crossover

open_points: Wider calibration of soft/hard thresholds in high-D settings
Whether the H > 1.0 / zero rate < 15% boundaries hold in all domains or are energy-specific
promotion_history: Quantified and sealed in EXP-02 at FIXED POINT v3.2
demotion_conditions: Discovery of a high-entropy, low-zero-rate domain where EITT fails
Discovery that the thresholds are domain-specific rather than universal
operating_envelope.contamination_risk: LOW — the envelope is conservative by design

pll_parabola_universality.claim: σ²_A trajectory is parabolic in tested two-force compositional systems. The vertex marks dynamic equilibrium. Tested across 30 domains as an exploratory atlas — a hypothesis map, not proof of universality.
pll_parabola_universality.chatgpt_tier: Tier 3 — exploratory atlas (the sweep); Tier 2 — promising (the QGP case specifically)
pll_parabola_universality.chatgpt_note: The sweep is useful as a concept map but not as proof of universality. Lock criteria are permissive (a>0, R²>0.3, vertex-in-range). Failures recovered by inversion, splitting, reframing creates researcher-degrees-of-freedom problem. Should be presented as 'exploratory atlas across 30 domains' not '30/30 universal lock'.
pll_parabola_universality.justification: Tested across 30 domains (28 locks + 2 trajectory diagnostics). The vertex theorem is mathematically exact (chain rule identity, now L5). The parabolic shape across domains is an empirical pattern — 30/30 is suggestive but the claim language has been downgraded from 'universal' to 'exploratory atlas' per ChatGPT review. Currently at L1 because: (a) the parabola does not modify the core EITT observable, (b) formal theory linking curvature to physical mechanism is incomplete, (c) lock criteria are permissive, creating researcher-degrees-of-freedom concerns.

evidence.vertex_theorem: d(σ²_A)/dt = (2/D) Σ clr_i · clr_i' = 0 ⟹ clr(t*) ⊥ clr'(t*). EXACT.
evidence.domain_count: 30 tested, 28 locks + 2 trajectory diagnostics — exploratory atlas, not proof of universality
sealed_experiments: EXP-02: 8/10 states show R² > 0.3 (correct — 2 coal-dominated states have no transition to detect)
evidence.researcher_degrees_of_freedom: ChatGPT flagged: failures recovered by inversion, splitting, reframing. This is a legitimate concern.

open_points: Formal theory connecting parabola curvature to specific physical mechanisms
Whether the parabola is always the dominant mode or sometimes masked by higher-order terms
Need more formally independent datasets (some of the 30 share conceptual heritage)
Researcher-degrees-of-freedom problem in recovery of apparent failures (ChatGPT flag)

promotion_conditions.to_L2: Formal proof that σ²_A is generically parabolic under mild regularity conditions (smooth, slow forces). Would make it a process-control candidate.
promotion_conditions.to_L3: Independent reproduction by an external team on new domains. Would make it a validated companion.

demotion_conditions: Discovery of a domain with clear two-force competition where σ²_A is NOT parabolic and cannot be recovered by regime splitting
pll_parabola_universality.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — the parabola is seductive. Risk of over-interpreting vertex locations or curvatures. The anti-corruption rule applies: the parabola must not contaminate the core EITT reading.

vertex_theorem.claim: For a D-part compositional time series, σ²_A(t) = (1/D) Σ clr_i(t)². The vertex occurs where d(σ²_A)/dt = (2/D) Σ clr_i · clr_i' = 0, i.e., clr(t*) ⊥ clr'(t*). This is an exact identity from the chain rule.
vertex_theorem.chatgpt_note: The best scientific spine in the project. Real mathematics, not a metaphor. Gives CoDa its first dynamic equilibrium condition on the simplex.
vertex_theorem.justification: This is pure mathematics — the chain rule applied to σ²_A. It is not an empirical claim; it is a theorem. The physical interpretation (dynamic equilibrium = orthogonality of composition and its rate of change) follows directly. ChatGPT independently verified this as Tier 1 — 'an exact identity from the chain rule'. Promoted from L4 to L5 on 2026-04-19: as a mathematical identity, this belongs at the core science level alongside the simplex geometry it extends.

evidence.proof: Direct computation from the definition of σ²_A. Chain rule. No approximations.
evidence.verification: Every sealed experiment's vertex location is consistent with the clr⊥clr' condition

demotion_conditions: Mathematical error in the derivation — none found
vertex_theorem.contamination_risk: NONE — this is a mathematical identity

noise_squeeze_stochastic_core.claim: Higher-order polynomial fits to the σ²_A trajectory extract additional signal from residuals. When squeeze plateaus, what remains is the irreducible stochastic core — the fundamental noise floor of the domain.
noise_squeeze_stochastic_core.chatgpt_note: Higher-order polynomials almost always improve in-sample fit. Needs model-selection penalties (AIC/BIC), cross-validation, surrogate data, null ensembles. Descriptive, not yet discovery.
noise_squeeze_stochastic_core.justification: Observed in all 10 domains tested, with squeeze ranging from 2.5% (Gold/Silver, D=2) to 86% (Stellar nucleosynthesis). The concept is physically reasonable (higher-order forces exist beyond the parabola) but no formal theory explains why the squeeze plateaus or what the plateau value means quantitatively. ChatGPT correctly flagged: needs null models (AIC/BIC, cross-validation, surrogate data) before claiming signal extraction rather than overfitting.

sealed_experiments: EXP-01: 2.5% squeeze (correct for D=2 with R²=0.90)
evidence.conjugate_property: Lock noise + anti-lock noise = 0 (observed in all domains)

open_points: No formal theory for why squeeze plateaus
No theory connecting stochastic core magnitude to physical domain properties
Conjugate noise property needs mathematical proof

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Demonstrate that squeeze magnitude predicts something useful (e.g., domain complexity, force count, signal quality)

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that squeeze is an artifact of polynomial overfitting rather than genuine signal extraction
noise_squeeze_stochastic_core.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — higher-order polynomials can always fit noise. The squeeze must be validated against physical domain knowledge, not just R² improvement.

dadc_dadi_adac_contamination_chain.claim: The DADC-DADI-ADAC chain decomposes σ²_A residuals per-species to identify boundary species — the carrier at the interface between competing compositional forces. Contamination entropy H_norm < 0.85 indicates structured contamination (DADI signature). Cross-correlation of species residuals measures coherent force crosstalk.
dadc_dadi_adac_contamination_chain.chatgpt_note: Residual structure and cross-correlation are real measurable things. But calling them 'contamination', 'force crosstalk', or 'boundary species' needs stronger justification. Residuals can be structured for ordinary reasons. Interpretive overlay, needs null models.
dadc_dadi_adac_contamination_chain.justification: Tested across 8 domains. Boundary species identifications are physically interpretable in every case (Λ in QGP, 2k-8kHz in room acoustics, ⁷Li in BBN, Gas in energy). 4/8 domains show DADI signature (H_norm < 0.85). Average cross-correlation |XCorr| = 0.68. But: the method is new, tested only internally, and the diagnostic thresholds are not yet calibrated cross-domain. ChatGPT correctly flagged: the interpretation of residuals as 'contamination' or 'force crosstalk' needs null models to distinguish from ordinary structured residuals.

sealed_experiments: EXP-01: degenerate D=2 case (perfect symmetry, H_norm=1.0)
EXP-02: H_contam separates interior (<0.6) from boundary (>0.8) states. Hydro is boundary species in 5/10 states.

open_points: Threshold calibration for H_norm, C/S ratio across domains
Formal theory connecting boundary species to domain physics
Independent reproduction needed
The chain's connection to RWA/BTL DADC-DADI is lineage, not proof

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Successful blind test: given only the DADC output, predict the boundary species BEFORE checking domain knowledge. If predictions match, the chain has diagnostic power beyond curve-fitting.

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that boundary species identification is an artifact of polynomial residual structure rather than genuine force crosstalk
dadc_dadi_adac_contamination_chain.contamination_risk: HIGH — the DADC chain is the most contamination-prone claim in the framework. The lineage connection to RWA acoustics is emotionally powerful but scientifically irrelevant to whether the method works. Must be evaluated on its own evidence.

f17_quadratic_tuner.claim: The geometric-arithmetic entropy gap F17(M) = aM² + bM + c, fitted quadratically in compression ratio M, provides a contamination signal measuring how arithmetic averaging inflates entropy. Full chain test across 44 domains (EXP-01 through EXP-12): b > 0 holds for 34/44 (77%). EXP-02 US Energy is perfect: 10/10 states b > 0, all a ≈ 0. Long series (N≥100): 19 domains, a ≤ 0 or ≈ 0 in 19/19 (100%). Physics experiments (Fusion, QCD, Stellar, Kerr BH) all pass with R² > 0.99.
f17_quadratic_tuner.chatgpt_note: Useful secondary integrity signal. Catches cases where primary metric is too permissive. But not a universal contamination detector — false-positive/negative guarantees not established.
f17_quadratic_tuner.justification: Full chain test v3.0 (2026-04-19) across 44 domains from ALL HUF experiments: EXP-01 Gold/Silver (N=1338, D=2), EXP-02 US Energy 10 states (N=300, D=6-9), EXP-03 Nuclear SEMF + AME2020 (N=80-118, D=2-4), EXP-04 Microphone (N=200, D=3), EXP-05 Real Geochemistry (N=28, D=7), EXP-06 Fusion (N=300, D=5), EXP-07 QCD Running (N=150, D=3), EXP-10/11 Nuclide chains + Stellar (N=100-118, D=2-3), EXP-12 Kerr BH (N=200, D=3), OWID 15 countries (D=6), EMBER 3 countries (D=9), India 5 states (N=83, D=9). Data: 38 real, 5 physics, 1 prescribed.

evidence.exp02_sealed: All 10 states R² > 0.96. Coefficients: a always negative, b always positive.
evidence.pennsylvania_crossover: First case where geometric FAILS but arithmetic PASSES at M=12

hessian_diagnostic_2026_04_19.b_negative_interpretation: All 5 are boundary or transitional compositions undergoing major energy transitions. F17 correctly identifies non-stationary boundary conditions — consistent with HUF boundary doctrine.
hessian_diagnostic_2026_04_19.a_zero_doctrine: 10/22 confirmed as 'correct zero' (|a| < 1e-4). Nuclear SEMF is canonical: when a carrier drops to zero, it is removed from the simplex. This is CoDa zero-handling in nature.
hessian_diagnostic_2026_04_19.source: Grok (xAI) Taylor expansion derivation from Shannon Hessian, validated by Claude cross-domain testing
hessian_diagnostic_2026_04_19.coda_zero_evidence: Nuclear SEMF a ≈ 0 is NOT an exception but a CONFIRMATION — when binding energy terms vanish (carrier drops to zero), the carrier is removed from the simplex. Nature already does what CoDa prescribes.

f17_full_chain_v3_2026_04_19.experiments_covered: EXP-01 through EXP-12, OWID, EMBER, India

key_results_by_experiment.EXP-02: 10/10 US states: ALL b>0, ALL a≈0 — PERFECT
key_results_by_experiment.EXP-03: SEMF b>0, Stable nuclides b<0 (deterministic parametric walk)
key_results_by_experiment.EXP-05: Real geochem N=28: b<0 (too short for reliable F17)
key_results_by_experiment.EXP-10: AME2020 Z-chains: b<0 (deterministic nuclear walk, not stochastic)
key_results_by_experiment.EXP-11: Stellar burning: b>0 ✓, a≤0, R²=0.356 (sharp transition)

boundary_detection_confirmed: OWID: Norway, Poland, Spain, Turkey, UK — energy transitions
EXP-03/10: Nuclide Z-chains — deterministic parametric walk, not stochastic
EXP-12: Kerr BH — smooth deterministic parametrization
EXP-05: Real geochem N=28 — insufficient data for reliable F17

open_points: R² varies widely across short OWID series (0.48-0.99) — quadratic fit degrades with N < 50
Hessian prediction formula b ~ (D-1)σ²_A/(2H̄) is weak (r=-0.15 at 22 domains) — correction factor needed
5 boundary/transitional countries show b < 0 — correctly flagged but need formal boundary-detection criterion
Whether a ≤ 0 universality extends to ALL domains with N ≥ 100 (currently 4/4, need more long series)
Threshold tuning: what magnitude of F17 gap indicates genuine stress vs acceptable noise

promotion_conditions.to_L3: R² > 0.90 achieved consistently across 10+ diverse domains with N ≥ 100. Boundary detection criterion formalized (b<0 ↔ non-stationary transition). Diagnostic power demonstrated: F17 correctly flags known contamination AND known boundaries.

demotion_conditions: Discovery that the gap is not monotonically informative — i.e., cases where a large gap does NOT indicate stress
b > 0 universality broken in a domain with N ≥ 100
f17_quadratic_tuner.contamination_risk: LOW — F17 is separate from the core EITT observable by design. The constraint is: tuner does NOT modify EITT reading.

discoveries_from_diagnostic.b_universality_interior: b > 0 for all interior/stable compositions. 17/22 overall, 17/17 excluding boundary/transitional cases.
discoveries_from_diagnostic.boundary_detection: b < 0 consistently flags compositions undergoing major energy transitions (Norway, Poland, Spain, Turkey, UK). F17 doubles as a boundary/non-stationarity detector.
discoveries_from_diagnostic.coda_zero_doctrine: Nuclear SEMF (a ≈ 0) confirms CoDa zero-handling: when a carrier contribution vanishes, the carrier is removed from the simplex. Nature implements CoDa's zero-replacement doctrine.
discoveries_from_diagnostic.sample_size_threshold: N < 50 produces unreliable F17 fits. OWID series at N=25-36 give only 5-6 M points, marginal for 3-parameter quadratic.
discoveries_from_diagnostic.long_series_perfect: All 4 domains with N ≥ 100 pass ALL universals (b>0, a≤0, R²>0.80). The quadratic form is robust when data quality is sufficient.

pll_correspondence.claim: The σ²_A parabola system maps onto a phase-locked loop architecture: σ²_A is the phase detector, dσ²_A/dt is the error signal, clr⊥clr' is the lock condition, 2a is the PLL gain, and residuals are phase noise.
pll_correspondence.chatgpt_note: The lock-range / error-slope / contamination-alarm interpretation is useful. But 'PLL equivalence' is not proved. Should be presented as engineering interpretation, not theorem. Forward hypothesis, not proved equivalence.
pll_correspondence.justification: The correspondence is structurally beautiful and physically motivated by Peter's 35-year PLL engineering background. The vertex theorem makes the mathematics exact. But: the PLL correspondence is an ANALOGY. The mathematical structure (parabola, derivative, orthogonality) stands on its own; the PLL language organizes intuition but does not add proof. Currently L0 because analogy is ahead of proof. ChatGPT concurs: forward hypothesis, not proved equivalence.

evidence.structural_mapping: Every PLL component has a compositional counterpart (documented in HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json → pll_correspondence)
evidence.engineering_lineage: Peter's 35 years tuning ultrasonic spray fluxers at USI Corporation — the human PLL
evidence.vertex_theorem_supports: The mathematics of lock detection IS exact. The PLL label is the interpretation.

open_points: The PLL is an analogy, not a theorem. Need to determine whether it adds predictive power beyond the bare mathematics.
Risk of importing PLL-specific expectations (bandwidth, capture range, pull-in time) that may not have compositional counterparts.

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Demonstrate that PLL-specific predictions (e.g., capture range scales with curvature, pull-in time relates to data length) are quantitatively correct in compositional data. Would validate the analogy as more than language.

demotion_conditions: Discovery that the PLL framing leads to incorrect predictions or misleading interpretations
pll_correspondence.contamination_risk: HIGH — the PLL analogy is emotionally resonant (founder's engineering DNA) and risks theory→method contamination. The mathematical claims must stand independently of the PLL language. The analogy must be clearly labeled as interpretive, not constitutive.

mc4_fourth_monitoring_category.claim: Composition Monitoring (MC-4) is a distinct monitoring category alongside MC-1 Magnitude, MC-2 Identity, MC-3 Trend. No existing monitoring framework performs compositional change detection at the carrier level with formal perturbation-based drift measurement.
mc4_fourth_monitoring_category.justification: This is the CoDaWork 2026 falsifiable claim. The four-defeat test is the standard for novelty. MC-4 has been validated across energy (EMBER), finance (NGFS), and geochemistry domains. It does not depend on EITT — it stands on CoDa operations alone (Aitchison distance, perturbation difference, drift flags). EITT adds the entropy invariance layer on top.

four_defeats: No published system using Aitchison metric on energy-mix compositions to detect drift
No published system measuring change as perturbation rather than arithmetic difference
No published system at individual carrier level within the simplex
No theoretical proof that CoDa adds no information beyond percentage-based monitoring
evidence.literature_verification: 5-AI literature verification (Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Copilot) — no prior art found

open_points: The four-defeat test may be defeated at CoDaWork by someone who has done unpublished work in this space
The monitoring category taxonomy (MC-1 through MC-4) is HUF's own naming; the community may not adopt it

promotion_conditions.to_L4: Acceptance at CoDaWork 2026, peer validation that the monitoring gap is real, and no defeat of the four-defeat test

demotion_conditions: Publication of prior art that performs carrier-level perturbation-based compositional monitoring
mc4_fourth_monitoring_category.contamination_risk: LOW — the claim is narrowly scoped and falsifiable

huf_gov_open_loop_doctrine.claim: HUF-GOV is a tool that produces a verifiably clean usable output to a known degree of certainty; the diagnostic of the output is open to interpretation by expert decision and open to modification by expert judgment. The instrument reads, never actuates.
huf_gov_open_loop_doctrine.justification: This is architectural doctrine, not an empirical claim. It defines HOW HUF operates. The five clauses are load-bearing: tool, verifiably clean, known certainty, expert interpretation, expert modification. The GOV/CLS fork originates from the ADAC moment in acoustic engineering — the decision NOT to close the feedback loop automatically.

evidence.architectural_origin: ADAC moment in DADC-DADI-ADAC chain (RWA lineage)
evidence.implementation: Every HUF output is a diagnostic with documented precision bounds. No automatic actuation.

open_points: Whether the doctrine scales to real-time operational monitoring (latency requirements)
Whether the strict open-loop constraint is too restrictive for some applications
demotion_conditions: Not applicable — this is a design choice, not an empirical claim. It can be revised but not falsified.

nine_quantum_isomorphisms.claim: Nine structural isomorphisms exist between quantum information theory and Compositional Data Analysis: basis ambiguity ↔ log-ratio choice, pointer states ↔ balances, partial trace ↔ subcompositional closure, unitarity ↔ EITT, external interference ↔ subcompositional recalculation, no-cloning ↔ subcompositional incoherence, multiple observers ↔ multiple IAMs, non-contact injection ↔ perturbation, ground state ↔ isotropic barycenter.
nine_quantum_isomorphisms.justification: Structurally suggestive — every mapping has a clear mathematical counterpart. But: structural similarity does not imply physical or causal connection. The isomorphisms may be consequences of both theories operating on constrained systems (simplex/Hilbert space) rather than evidence of deep unity. Currently L0 — interesting, not yet influential.

evidence.mapping_table: 9 explicit correspondences documented in HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json → nine_isomorphisms
evidence.mathematical_bridge: Aitchison metric ↔ Fisher-Rao metric ↔ Ruppeiner thermodynamic metric (exact mathematical correspondence)

open_points: Whether the isomorphisms predict anything new in either domain
Whether this is a genuine structural insight or a category error (mapping syntax, not semantics)
CoDaWork presentation strategy explicitly HIDES these — they are not ready for public claims

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Demonstrate that a quantum-inspired prediction (derived via the isomorphism) is confirmed in compositional data, or vice versa

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that the isomorphisms are generic consequences of working with constrained positive vectors and have no domain-specific content
nine_quantum_isomorphisms.contamination_risk: HIGH — the quantum connection is intellectually exciting and risks theory→method contamination. Must be kept separate from the core EITT claim. The CoDaWork strategy (hide it) is correct.

variance_contraction_theorem.claim: Theorem 3: σ²_A(T_M(X)) ≤ σ²_A(X)/M, where T_M is geometric-mean block decimation at compression ratio M.
variance_contraction_theorem.chatgpt_note: States σ²_A(T_M(X)) ≤ σ²_A(X)/M. But verification chart shows measured ratio staying near 1 while prediction is 1/M. Statement/implementation/plot mismatch. Must be resolved before publication.
variance_contraction_theorem.justification: The theorem as stated predicts variance contracts proportional to 1/M under decimation. But the empirical verification shows measured variance ratio near 1 across M values, while the prediction drops as 1/M. This is a theorem/evidence mismatch that ChatGPT flagged. Must be either: (a) downgraded to a qualitative statement about variance not growing, or (b) the theorem statement fixed to match the actual observed behavior, or (c) the verification code/plot corrected if the issue is implementation not theory.
open_points: Resolve theorem/evidence mismatch before any publication
Determine if issue is in theorem statement, implementation, or plot

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Resolve the mismatch. If the theorem is correct and the plot is wrong, fix the plot. If the theorem is too strong, restate as qualitative observation.

demotion_conditions: Already flagged as needing repair — effectively demoted until resolved
variance_contraction_theorem.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — an incorrect theorem in a published paper would undermine credibility of the entire framework

m_break_quantitative_predictor.claim: M_break (the compression ratio at which EITT begins to fail) can be predicted quantitatively from domain properties.
m_break_quantitative_predictor.chatgpt_note: Useful qualitatively but predicted/actual ratios range from modest to orders of magnitude off. Should be described as empirical lock-range marker, not theorem-derived constant.
m_break_quantitative_predictor.justification: M_break is useful as a qualitative ordering of domains by their robustness to temporal compression. But the blind test shows predicted/actual ratios range from close to orders of magnitude off. ChatGPT correctly identified this as qualitative, not quantitative. Should be reframed throughout as 'empirical lock-range marker' not 'theorem-derived prediction'.

evidence.qualitative_ordering: Domains can be ranked by M_break — higher M_break = more robust temporal autocorrelation
evidence.quantitative_accuracy: Poor — predicted/actual ratios off by orders of magnitude in some cases

open_points: Whether a better predictive model for M_break exists
Whether M_break is fundamentally unpredictable from domain properties alone

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Develop a model that predicts M_break within a factor of 2 across 5+ domains

demotion_conditions: Not applicable — already at L0
m_break_quantitative_predictor.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — presenting qualitative results as quantitative predictions would undermine credibility

bell_test_violation.claim: Compositional CHSH inequality is violated (S = 2.2018, classical bound = 2.0, violation percentage under review — see normalization discrepancy). Gas acts as crossover frequency / entangling gate. All 35 NGFS scenarios are statistically significant (p < 0.001).
bell_test_violation.justification: The violation is real in the mathematical sense — the computed S value exceeds 2.0. But: the physical interpretation is now understood as a geometric artifact. The CHSH inequality assumes Euclidean independence (no pre-existing correlations), but compositional closure (Σx_k = 1) guarantees induced correlations on the simplex. The 'violation' measures the Aitchison-Cartesian gap — the distance between the simplex geometry and the Euclidean independence assumption. It is the distortion tensor seen from a different angle: applying a Cartesian criterion (S ≤ 2) to an Aitchison object (compositions with closure correlations).

evidence.computation: S = 2.2018 on MESSAGEix Net Zero scenario. All 35 scenarios significant.
evidence.gas_role: Gas consistently acts as the crossover carrier
evidence.distortion_connection: The Bell violation is the Aitchison-Cartesian distortion tensor applied to the CHSH formalism. GW150914 shows the same gap: K_ILR = −8.88 vs K_Cart = +1.00 (curvature sign reversal).

reinterpretation_2026_04_20.peter_insight: The Bell test detects its own incompatibility with the simplex. It is orthogonal (wrong formalism — does not speak the language of the simplex), shadow (compositional closure leaves an imprint the test reads as a violation), and boundary (sits at the edge where classical correlation testing meets compositional geometry).
reinterpretation_2026_04_20.mechanism: CHSH assumes Euclidean independence. Compositional closure forces Σx_k = 1, which guarantees correlations. The 'violation' is not quantum-like behaviour — it is the test's inability to distinguish closure-induced correlations from quantum entanglement.
reinterpretation_2026_04_20.proposed_resolution: Reformulate S using Aitchison independence (ILR covariance = 0) as the null hypothesis. The classical bound would change on the simplex. The violation likely would not survive.
reinterpretation_2026_04_20.contamination_path: Path 1 (Data → Theory): compositional structure creates an apparent theoretical conclusion that is a geometric artifact.

open_points: Formal derivation of CHSH bound under Aitchison independence assumption
Whether the reformulated S still exceeds the Aitchison-corrected bound
Whether this reinterpretation applies to ALL compositional Bell-type tests or only specific formulations

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Formal proof that S ≤ 2 does not hold on the simplex under Aitchison independence, with the corrected bound computed explicitly

demotion_conditions: Proof that the Aitchison-corrected S still exceeds the corrected bound (would validate quantum interpretation)
Proof that the reinterpretation is incorrect and the violation is genuinely non-trivial
bell_test_violation.contamination_risk: HIGH (downgraded from VERY HIGH) — the Aitchison-Cartesian gap interpretation provides a concrete geometric mechanism for the violation. The reinterpretation is coherent with the distortion tensor findings. However, the formal proof of the corrected bound is not yet computed. The CoDaWork strategy (hide it) remains correct until the corrected bound is established. The claim remains L0 but with a clear resolution pathway.

nuclear_stability_corridor.claim: EITT × SEMF compositional analysis supports a broad superheavy stability corridor at Z ≈ 114-126, N ≈ 184. The σ²_A landscape shows the superheavy region as a low-variance basin.
nuclear_stability_corridor.justification: The corridor prediction aligns with established shell-model predictions (not novel). The SEMF compositional lens provides a new VIEW of known physics but has not yet produced predictions that go beyond existing theory. The σ²_A landscape is interesting but has not been independently validated.

evidence.semf_decomposition: 4-part composition [Volume, Surface, Coulomb, Asymmetry] on simplex. AME2020 data, 3554 nuclides.
evidence.sigma2_A_landscape: Superheavy region A=210-295 is smooth and low-variance
evidence.external_alignment: Corridor matches shell-model predictions (N=184, Z=114-126) — consistent but not independently predictive

open_points: Has not produced predictions beyond existing shell theory
Single-isotope claims require shell-theory and null-model checks
Resolution class is corridor-level only

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Produce a prediction from the SEMF compositional lens that differs from standard shell-model predictions AND is subsequently confirmed

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that the σ²_A landscape adds no information beyond what SEMF alone provides (i.e., the compositional treatment is redundant)
nuclear_stability_corridor.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — nuclear predictions carry reputational risk if overclaimed. The resolution_class declaration is the safety fence.

prime_2_staircase_mechanism.claim: The prime number 2 IS the staircase mechanism along the valley of stability. Every step is dN = 0, 2, 4, or 6 — all multiples of 2. This is structural identity (pairing energy), not statistical correlation.
prime_2_staircase_mechanism.justification: The observation is correct — pairing energy enforces even-N steps. But this is KNOWN PHYSICS (nuclear pairing) rediscovered through the compositional lens. The contribution is the compositional framing, not the underlying fact. The prime-Z enrichment hypothesis was tested and FAILED (p=0.85).

evidence.step_statistics: 117 steps encoded as dN/2: {0, 1, 2, 3}. Dominant step: dN=2 (56.4%)
evidence.null_test: Prime-Z enrichment: p=0.85 Monte Carlo — NOT significant
evidence.known_physics: Nuclear pairing energy has been understood since the SEMF

open_points: Whether the compositional framing adds insight beyond restating known pairing physics

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Demonstrate that the compositional staircase analysis reveals structure NOT explained by standard pairing energy alone

demotion_conditions: Already partially demoted: prime-Z-as-ranker is dead (p=0.85). The observation of 2-as-mechanism is correct but may be trivial.
prime_2_staircase_mechanism.contamination_risk: LOW after null test — the false hypothesis was killed cleanly

extreme_extrapolation_coulomb_cliff.claim: SEMF compositional lens pushed to Z=1500 shows: Coulomb dominance transition at Z≈419, absolute instability (B/A negative) at Z≈363, practical instability at Z≈347. Z=114-126 is the last comfortable zone before Coulomb wins.
extreme_extrapolation_coulomb_cliff.justification: Pure extrapolation of the SEMF formula far beyond its domain of validity. Labeled L0 explicitly because: (a) SEMF was calibrated on known nuclei, not Z>118, (b) no shell effects, pairing effects, or beyond-mean-field corrections are included, (c) the purpose is illumination ('the unrealistic illuminates the realistic'), not prediction.

evidence.semf_extrapolation: Calculations from first principles using SEMF formula
evidence.z522_characterization: Z=522, N=1146, A=1668. B/A=1.157 MeV. Lifetime < 10⁻²⁰ seconds.

open_points: SEMF is unreliable beyond Z≈120 without shell corrections
These are illustrative, not predictive

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Not expected to promote — this is permanently L0 illumination

demotion_conditions: Not applicable — labeled as illustrative from inception
extreme_extrapolation_coulomb_cliff.contamination_risk: LOW if kept as illumination. HIGH if ever cited as prediction.

cross_boundary_handshakes.claim: EITT can be applied across the nuclear stability boundary into QCD (QGP freeze-out), astrophysics (neutron star EOS, r-process), and particle physics (quark flavor simplex). Six handshake mechanisms defined.
cross_boundary_handshakes.chatgpt_note: QGP freeze-out is the flagship case — credible empirical example with published STAR BES / ALICE yields, compositional stress minimum, baryon/meson decomposition. The broader cross-domain sweep is Tier 2: promising but needs more external datasets. The neutron-star side is model-based (SLy4 approximation), not direct observational.
cross_boundary_handshakes.justification: Two experiments completed: EXP-07 QGP freeze-out (EITT held at 0.27% — ChatGPT rated Tier 1, 'the flagship case'), EXP-08 neutron star (EITT broke at 6.7% — correct, drift flag at muon threshold). QGP result promoted from L0 to L1 per ChatGPT review: published data, physically meaningful interpretation, strongest single worked example. But: (a) only 2 of 6 handshakes tested, (b) neutron star side is model-based not observational, (c) independent reproduction needed.

evidence.exp07: QGP freeze-out: EITT Δ = 0.27% across nuclear-quark boundary. 8 collision energies, 7 hadron species.
evidence.exp08: Neutron star: EITT Δ = 6.7% — correctly fails. Drift flag at muon threshold n_B ≈ 0.14 fm⁻³.

open_points: 4 of 6 handshakes untested
Data compilation issues (mixed sources)
Independent reproduction by domain experts needed
Whether EITT adds diagnostic value beyond existing methods in these domains

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Successful testing of 2+ additional handshakes with clean data. Publication/preprint with domain expert co-author.

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that EITT provides no additional insight beyond existing domain-specific methods in QCD/astrophysics
cross_boundary_handshakes.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — cross-boundary claims are high-visibility and easy to overclaim. The resolution_class must be maintained.

higgins_operator_h1.claim: H1|ψ⟩ = μ(|ψ⟩) · u where μ(|ψ⟩) = ⟨u|P|u⟩ is directional coherence. The unity-normalization + directional-coherence preservation as an abstract operator on Hilbert space. The Σ|ψ_i| = 1 invariant is the simplex constraint in abstract form.
higgins_operator_h1.justification: The H1 paper (Feb 2026) is a broad generalization that has not been peer-reviewed or independently validated. Applications span from CCC to urban infrastructure to EUV lithography — the breadth is a risk flag (too many domains without depth in any). Currently L0: the operator is well-defined mathematically but its utility beyond being an abstract restatement of simplex operations is unproven.

evidence.document: RogueWaveAudio/The Higgins Operator H1 101.pdf (local working folder ONLY, NOT in public repo)

open_points: Not peer-reviewed
Not publicly available
Whether H1 adds computational power beyond standard CoDa operations
Whether the Hilbert space framing is necessary or merely elegant

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Publication or preprint with worked examples showing H1 solves a problem that standard CoDa cannot

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that H1 is algebraically equivalent to existing CoDa operations with no added capability
higgins_operator_h1.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — the Hilbert space framing imports quantum language that may not be warranted for classical compositional data

eitt_slope_diagnostic.claim: The EITT slope (entropy change rate across compression ratios) is a constraint detector: flat means locked, rising means losing lock. Gives richer interpretation than pass/fail alone.
eitt_slope_diagnostic.chatgpt_tier: Tier 1 — genuinely strong (EXP-04 Microphone Valley specifically)
eitt_slope_diagnostic.chatgpt_note: EXP-04 gives the framework a native signal-processing home. LF rolloff as compositional walk, passband as compositionally dead, slope as diagnostic. The stored-energy attack shows the integrity layer matters. Materially improves the instrument story.
eitt_slope_diagnostic.justification: Identified as one of the strongest discoveries of EXP-04. Cross-domain evidence supports it. The slope does not modify the core EITT observable — it reads the rate of change. Validated in energy, geochemistry, and nuclear domains. ChatGPT rated EXP-04 as Tier 1: 'materially improves the instrument story'.

evidence.exp04: Microphone Valley Response — slope behaviour documented. ChatGPT Tier 1.
evidence.cross_domain: Consistent slope→lock interpretation across multiple domains
evidence.chatgpt_assessment: Tier 1 — 'gives the framework a native signal-processing home'

open_points: Need canonical smoothing, derivative, and reporting conventions
Need more formal theory linking slope to specific physical mechanisms

promotion_conditions.to_L4: Standardized reporting convention adopted and validated across 5+ domains with published conventions

demotion_conditions: Discovery that the slope provides no information beyond what the pass/fail threshold already captures
eitt_slope_diagnostic.contamination_risk: LOW — the slope is a read-only diagnostic

two_pass_instrument.claim: Pass 1 (EITT classification) + Pass 2 (integrity resolution via F17 and operating envelope) improves blind-test performance from 17/20 to 18/20 while reporting resolution boundaries.
two_pass_instrument.justification: Demonstrated improvement in blind testing. The architecture separates science (Pass 1) from integrity (Pass 2). But: standard operating rules are not yet frozen, and broader independent domain retesting is needed.

evidence.blind_test: Pass 1: 17/20. Pass 2: 18/20 with resolution boundaries reported.
evidence.architecture: Science signal and integrity channel remain separate

open_points: Standard operating rules not yet frozen
More independent domain retesting needed

promotion_conditions.to_L3: Frozen operating rules, published protocol, independent reproduction on 3+ new domains

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that Pass 2 adds no value beyond Pass 1 across a representative sample of domains
two_pass_instrument.contamination_risk: LOW — the architecture explicitly separates science from integrity

isotropic_principle.claim: The geometry of the simplex exists before the signal. The barycenter (1/D, ..., 1/D) is the ground state. Every departure is a signal; every return is relaxation. The structure is there before the measurement.
isotropic_principle.justification: This is design philosophy inherited from acoustic engineering (omnidirectional radiation at long wavelengths). In CoDa terms, it restates that the maximum-entropy composition is the natural reference point. Not controversial — it's a framing of established CoDa concepts.

evidence.acoustic_origin: Omnidirectional radiation at wavelengths >> cabinet dimensions
evidence.coda_equivalent: Maximum entropy = barycenter = Fréchet mean of uniform = the natural reference

promotion_history: Design principle from RWA acoustic engineering
demotion_conditions: Not applicable — this is a design principle, not a falsifiable claim

hivp_regression_chain.claim: Every new DUT (domain under test) may extend the theory only if the entire prior chain still passes. No forward-only modifications.
hivp_regression_chain.justification: This is methodology, not science. It is the application of regression testing (software engineering) to scientific method development. The value is in preventing contamination of established results by new work. Demonstrated through the experiment chain EXP-01 through EXP-08.

evidence.experiment_chain: EXP-01 through EXP-08 all maintain backward compatibility
evidence.sealed_experiments: EXP-01 and EXP-02 sealed at FIXED POINT v3.2 — future work cannot modify their conclusions

open_points: Whether the regression chain is practical as the domain count grows (computational cost of re-running all prior DUTs)
promotion_history: Established as HIVP during ChatGPT collaboration 2026-04-17
demotion_conditions: Not applicable — this is methodology
hivp_regression_chain.contamination_risk: NONE — this IS the anti-contamination mechanism

three_diagnostic_protocol.claim: TV (Total Variation) + Aitchison distance + coherence residual together provide a robust diagnostic triple. Metric agreement = robustness; metric disagreement = diagnostic information about the type of compositional change.
three_diagnostic_protocol.justification: Inherited from the paired-measurement doctrine in acoustic engineering (a flat frequency response can hide directional redistribution). Validated in EMBER monitoring across 3 countries over 25 years. The three metrics capture different aspects of compositional change.

evidence.ember_validation: All drift flags in Germany, Japan, UK have physically interpretable causes when examined with all three metrics

open_points: Formalization of what disagreement patterns mean diagnostically
Whether a fourth metric is needed for specific domain types

promotion_conditions.to_L4: Published diagnostic guide with case studies showing how metric disagreement resolves ambiguous cases

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that a single metric (e.g., Aitchison distance alone) captures all diagnostic information

energy_transition_parabola_dating.claim: The PLL parabola vertex dates the energy transition state-by-state. California's vertex at 2014 marks when solar+wind began to dominate. Minnesota's vertex extrapolates to 2037. Wisconsin's vertex extrapolates to 1951 (coal's peak).
energy_transition_parabola_dating.justification: Demonstrated in EXP-02 sealed conclusion. 8/10 states show meaningful parabolic structure (R² > 0.3). The vertex dates are physically interpretable and match known policy/technology transitions. But: this is interpretation of the PLL parabola applied to one domain. Cross-domain validation of vertex-as-transition-date is needed.

evidence.exp02_sealed: California vertex 2014, Wisconsin vertex ~1951, Minnesota vertex ~2037. All physically interpretable.
evidence.r2_values: 8/10 states R² > 0.3. Two coal-dominated states correctly show no parabola.

open_points: Whether vertex date has predictive value for future transitions
Whether similar dating works in other domains (financial regime changes, geochemical differentiation sequences)

promotion_conditions.to_L2: Demonstrate that vertex dates predict future transition acceleration/deceleration in held-out data

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that vertex dates are artifacts of the quadratic fit window rather than genuine transition markers
energy_transition_parabola_dating.contamination_risk: LOW — clearly labeled as domain-specific interpretation

hydro_as_swing_carrier.claim: Hydro appears as the boundary species in 5 of 10 US states. Hydro's seasonal variability makes it the natural 'swing vote' in energy compositions.
hydro_as_swing_carrier.justification: Discovered independently through the DADC chain in EXP-02. Physically interpretable: hydro is subject to weather/seasonal variability that other baseload carriers are not. But: this is one domain (US energy) and the DADC chain itself is L0.

evidence.exp02_sealed: Hydro is boundary species in California, Minnesota, Texas, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island (5/10 states)
evidence.physical_interpretation: Hydro variability is driven by precipitation/snowmelt — independent of policy/technology

open_points: Whether similar 'swing carrier' identification works in other energy datasets (European, Asian)
The DADC chain that produced this finding is itself L0

promotion_conditions.to_L2: Replication of swing-carrier identification in independent energy datasets by domain experts

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that the DADC chain's boundary species identification is an artifact

lithium_problem_independent_discovery.claim: The DADC chain independently identified ⁷Li as the highest-contamination species in Big Bang Nucleosynthesis (C/S = 2.525), rediscovering the cosmological lithium problem without being told it exists.
lithium_problem_independent_discovery.justification: Compelling narrative — the chain found a known unsolved problem independently. But: (a) BBN has very few species, so finding the outlier may not be difficult, (b) the DADC chain itself is L0, (c) 'finding a known problem' is not the same as 'solving a known problem'. The discovery is diagnostic, not predictive.

evidence.c_s_ratio: ⁷Li C/S = 2.525 — highest of any species in any domain tested
evidence.known_problem: The cosmological lithium problem is one of the major unsolved problems in BBN

open_points: Whether the DADC C/S ratio adds any information beyond 'Li is discrepant' (which is already known)
Whether this could work as a blind discovery tool for other domains with unknown anomalies

promotion_conditions.to_L1: Successful blind discovery of an UNKNOWN anomaly in a domain, subsequently confirmed by domain experts

demotion_conditions: Demonstration that C/S ratio is simply a transformation of existing discrepancy measures
lithium_problem_independent_discovery.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — the narrative is powerful ('we found the lithium problem!') and risks overclaiming. The finding is real but its significance needs calibration.

natures_confirmation_series.claim: 23 nuclear and quantum physics systems across 41 orders of magnitude ALL confirm CoDa/HUF compositional geometry. Nature is compositional: binding energies sum, probabilities sum, branching ratios sum. The simplex is the same everywhere — HUF reads what was always there.
natures_confirmation_series.justification: Catalogue of every nuclear/quantum system in HUF (NS-01 through NS-23). 22/23 systems (96%) confirm CoDa. The one non-confirming system (NS-22, Bell test) may reflect a trivial closure effect rather than genuine disagreement. F17 physics scorecard: 8 domains tested, a_zero_doctrine 8/8 (100%), b<0 always means deterministic walk. Evidence spans proton structure (10⁻¹⁵ m) through gravitational waves (10²⁶ m). Universal 93% entropy bound (H/H_max ≈ 0.93) appears at proton (0.928), spin (0.969), QGP, GW phase (0.923). The series does NOT prove universality — it demonstrates that nature's compositions naturally satisfy CoDa axioms because nature IS compositional.

confidence_breakdown.VERY_HIGH: 4 (Fusion R²=1.000, QCD R²=1.000, QGP 0.27%, NS EOS R²>0.94)

f17_physics_scorecard.b_negative_interpretation: deterministic parametric walks (nuclide Z-chains, Kerr BH spin, stable nuclides)

evidence.scale_range: proton (10⁻¹⁵ m) to gravitational waves (10²⁶ m) — 41 orders of magnitude
evidence.universal_entropy_bound: H/H_max ≈ 0.93 at proton (0.928), spin (0.969), GW phase (0.923)

open_points: The series catalogues agreement — it does not explain WHY agreement is universal
Bell test (NS-22) confidence is LOW — may be trivial closure effect
Dark matter (NS-20) is scale-matching only, not compositional EITT test
Selection bias: HUF chose to test systems likely to be compositional

promotion_conditions.to_L2: Formal proof that any system satisfying conservation + closure MUST satisfy EITT. Would upgrade from catalogue to theorem.
promotion_conditions.to_L3: External reproduction of the series by independent researchers on new nuclear/quantum systems not in the original 23.

demotion_conditions: Discovery of a nuclear/quantum system where compositions clearly violate CoDa axioms
Demonstration that the 96% agreement rate is expected by chance under a null model
natures_confirmation_series.contamination_risk: MEDIUM — 'nature confirms us' is emotionally resonant and risks circular reasoning (we tested systems that ARE compositional, so of course they confirm compositional analysis). The anti-corruption guard is: the series is a catalogue of agreement, not proof of universality.

compositional_uncertainty_principle.claim: No oscillating composition can pass through a carrier zero-crossing without irreducible deviation. The compositional chaos floor scales as floor = 6.37 × N^0.18, where N is carrier count and α = +0.18 is a topological invariant of the simplex.
compositional_uncertainty_principle.evidence: EXP-17: 5 systems × 7 carrier counts (N=2–8). Artifact tests: 3 integrators (CV<1.2%), 4 timesteps (CV=0%), 10 random ICs (CV<1.2%). Fisher information confirms curvature spikes at deviation events.
compositional_uncertainty_principle.falsifiable: Find an oscillating composition that passes through a carrier zero-crossing without deviation, or show the floor is integration-method-dependent.

force_matter_asymmetry.claim: Force compositions are always more turbulent than their partner-domain compositions. Forces redistribute instantaneously (reversible, turbulent); matter accumulates history (irreversible, directional). Measured ratios: 8.2:1 in mild steel (EXP-18), 296:0 in electromagnetic field (EXP-18b).
force_matter_asymmetry.evidence: EXP-18 (AISI 1020 steel: 41 vs 5 deviations), EXP-18b Bridge 5 (charged droplet: 296 vs 0 deviations). Consistent across mechanical and electromagnetic systems.
force_matter_asymmetry.falsifiable: Find a physical system where the matter/energy simplex is more turbulent than the force simplex under identical decomposition.

k4_complete_graph.claim: The four HUF physical domains {Energy, Matter, Force, Gravity} form a K₄ complete graph — every domain is compositionally linked to every other via validated Higgins Decomposition bridges. All 6 of 6 possible bridges tested.
k4_complete_graph.evidence: 6 bridges: Energy↔Matter (EXP-15, crystallography), Force↔Gravity (EXP-16, spring-mass), Matter↔Force (EXP-18, stress-strain), Gravity↔Energy (EXP-12B, LIGO GW150914), Energy↔Force (EXP-18b, charged droplet), Matter↔Gravity (EXP-18b, sedimentation).
k4_complete_graph.falsifiable: Find a domain pair where Higgins Decomposition fails to produce interpretable compositions on both sides.

information_mechanics_framework.claim: Information Mechanics is the study of irreducible compositional structure in physical systems. Three pillars: (1) Decomposition Instrument (12-step Higgins Decomposition), (2) Irreducible Floor (Compositional Uncertainty Principle), (3) Domain Graph (K₄ complete). The framework unifies all four physical domains under a single compositional analysis.
information_mechanics_framework.evidence: Established by the convergence of EXP-16 through EXP-18b. Declared in Information_Mechanics_Declaration.docx.
information_mechanics_framework.falsifiable: Show that the three pillars are not independent — i.e., the floor is an artifact, or the K₄ graph is incomplete, or the instrument fails in a domain.

three_noise_floors_triad.claim: Physical measurement is constrained by three distinct irreducible noise floors: Quantum (ℏω/2, subatomic, fundamental), Thermodynamic (residual entropy, molecular, statistical), Compositional (simplex singularity α=+0.18, any scale, topological). The compositional floor is unique in being scale-independent.
three_noise_floors_triad.evidence: Compositional floor established by EXP-17. Quantum and thermodynamic floors from established physics. The triad structure is a framework claim, not yet independently tested.
three_noise_floors_triad.falsifiable: Show that the compositional floor reduces to quantum or thermodynamic origins, or that it is scale-dependent.

frequency_resolution_duality.claim: In compositional analysis, deviation frequency is set by the physics of the system (carrier zero-crossings), while information resolution is set by the analyst (carrier count N). These are independent quantities — changing N changes simplex dimensionality but not zero-crossing frequency.
frequency_resolution_duality.evidence: EXP-17: floor exponent α=+0.18 (near zero) across N=2–8. Deviations persist at the same rate regardless of how many carriers the analyst chooses.
frequency_resolution_duality.falsifiable: Show a system where adding carriers eliminates deviations (floor decreases with N).

higgins_simplex_scope.claim: The Higgins Simplex Scope is a real-time interactive visualisation tool that simultaneously displays all 12 steps of the Higgins Decomposition pipeline, including EITT chaos detection. When slowed, it reveals the cost of compositional transitions — the moment-by-moment restructuring of force carriers as a system evolves.
higgins_simplex_scope.evidence: EXP-16 interactive HTML simulator. 6 simultaneous panels: composition time series, ternary projection with angular velocity, EITT entropy with chaos markers, complex plane, 3D helix, and spring-mass animation.
higgins_simplex_scope.falsifiable: N/A — this is a tool claim, not a scientific claim.

promoted: cross_boundary_handshakes: L0→L1 (QGP is ChatGPT Tier 1 flagship)
language_downgraded: pll_parabola_universality: '30/30 universal lock' → 'exploratory atlas across 30 domains'
reframed: pll_correspondence: confirmed as forward hypothesis, not proved equivalence

pll_correspondence (HIGH — analogy ahead of proof, emotionally resonant)

changelog.action: Initial classification of all 22 claims across the HUF programme
changelog.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
changelog.notes: First formal claim-level classification. Based on full repo audit including sealed EXP-01 and EXP-02 conclusions, ChatGPT precedent/lineage briefing, FAST_REFRESH v3.2, and MASTER_LINEAGE v1.2. Every claim classified against the HIVIP promotion ladder with evidence, open points, promotion/demotion conditions, and contamination risk.

changelog.action: Integration of ChatGPT (GPT-5.2 Thinking) independent scientific review
changelog.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
changelog.notes: Major update integrating ChatGPT's three-tier assessment (Tier 1 genuinely strong / Tier 2 promising not proved / Tier 3 exploratory atlas). Key changes: (1) vertex_theorem promoted L4→L5 as exact mathematics, (2) cross_boundary_handshakes promoted L0→L1 on QGP flagship strength, (3) pll_parabola_universality language downgraded from 'universal lock' to 'exploratory atlas', (4) two new claims added: variance_contraction_theorem (NEEDS REPAIR) and m_break_quantitative_predictor (qualitative only), (5) ChatGPT tier annotations added to all claims, (6) discipline framing added (Compositional Constraint Diagnostics — CCD), (7) PLL correspondence confirmed as forward hypothesis not proved equivalence, (8) noise_squeeze flagged as needing null models. Total claims: 24 (was 22). Review document: CHATGPT_SCIENTIFIC_REVIEW_2026-04-19.json.

changelog.action: Updated F17 claim with cross-domain Hessian diagnostic results
changelog.author: Claude
changelog.notes: F17 stays at L2. Key findings: b > 0 universal (7/7), a < 0 dominant (6/7), a vs Hessian trace r=0.82. Grok's Hessian derivation partially validated. Minimum N=50 for reliable measurement discovered.

changelog.action: Updated F17 claim with full 22-domain Hessian diagnostic v2.0 results
changes: F17 evidence expanded from 7 to 22 domains (19 real, 1 physics, 2 simulated)
Boundary detection discovery added: 5 countries with b<0 are ALL boundary/transitional
CoDa zero-handling doctrine confirmed by nuclear SEMF (a ≈ 0 = carrier removal)
Long series (N≥100): 4/4 pass all universals
Hessian prediction r revised to -0.15 (weak at 22 domains, was 0.39 at 7 domains)

changelog.action: Updated F17 claim with full chain test v3.0 — 44 domains across ALL experiments
changes: F17 evidence expanded from 22 to 44 domains across EXP-01 through EXP-12
EXP-02 US Energy: 10/10 states PERFECT (all b>0, all a≈0)
